# Anupam Sobti (Web Development Using AI)

<!-- vimeo: 1111477920 | track: English (auto-generated) -->

Hi everyone. I'm Dr. Anam ti, an assistant professor at Psha University in Punjab, India. I'm going to talk about my course on web development using ai, also known as web coding 1 0 1. With the increasing utility of large language models in modern software workflow, I envisioned integrating AI workflows into traditional web development for this course. Typically, students learn fundamentals such as data structures, programming, linear algebra, et cetera, in their early engineering years. However, this is a bottoms up approach towards building useful things with software, which leads to a relevance gap. In terms of the undergraduate students understanding with why Coding 1 0 1, I introduced students to the journey from ideas to prototypes. Each student proposed the ideas that they wanted to work on, including four components, basic web development, element integration, and drag and agent pipelines. With about 25, 20 5% selection rate of students with a mix of second and third year undergraduate students, we selected students who were highly motivated and had a clear vision of what they wanted to build. The prerequisite for the course was Python programming, which our students covered in their first semester. We built on this understanding to enable them to create API endpoints using Fast API. We introduced concepts right from basic S-T-M-L-C-S-S, JavaScript through databases, API, endpoints and security practices. While students were prompting their functionality, their ability to read and debug Python was very useful. We used prompting for everything from H-T-M-L-C-S-S, JavaScript generation to creating endpoints and connecting frontend and backend through calls. We used both tools such as Cursor, lovable Bolt, as well as API endpoints for reproducible controlled generation for things such as databases. Hi, I build Course five, an AI enhanced course Review and recommendation platform. This is primarily to be used by college students in India for selecting their courses every single semester. It made me realize that while AI can be treated as a specialized skill, you can also see it as a capability that can sort of be integrated into almost any project or domain. And so now instead of viewing coursework as isolated subjects, I see myself trying to identify various opportunities where you can sort of explore infusing ai. I would say the aha moment in my project would be just like when I was able to get the memory function functioning in my agent. So like they, I could remember the past conversation and also the past steps that it did earlier to actually like, uh, know better what the next step should be. The breakthrough for me came when I shifted from using GPT purely as an assistant in the browser to sort of hitting the API directly giving it, you know, my full con, full code based context. Because this shift essentially meant that it could understand my entire project structure and, you know, often like offer targeted fixes without my having to paste snippets back and forth. And that was sort of the aha moment for me when I realized truly, you know, what are some of the capabilities that can be unlocked using ai. What excites me most about this area is how quickly and easily an idea can move from a concept to a working prototype. And this speed of iteration is, you know, what makes it possible to test, refine, and even deploy ideas much, much faster than traditional methods and without that barrier that used to exist previously of domain knowledge, which I think is incredibly exciting. So in summary, I would say this course was an experiment in integrating AI to speed up your understanding of the entire software development stack. Students had a lot of fun.
